description:
You are acting as a penetration-tester who has already completed two reconnaissance phases:

1. An nmap TCP sweep that produced a CSV called  
   /home/user/scans/open_ports.csv  
2. A vulnerability look-up that produced a CSV called  
   /home/user/scans/vulns.csv  

Your goal is to extract very specific columns from each file, align them side-by-side with the paste utility (after you have used cut to isolate the needed columns), and write a consolidated report that relates every discovered open port to any known vulnerability for the corresponding service.

Files that already exist when the session starts
-------------------------------------------------
1. /home/user/scans/open_ports.csv  
   Column order (with header row):  
   IP,Port,Protocol,Service

2. /home/user/scans/vulns.csv  
   Column order (with header row):  
   Service,CVE,Severity

Exact column extraction requirements
------------------------------------
• From open_ports.csv you must keep ONLY the following two columns, **in this order**:  
  1) IP  
  2) Port  
  3) Service  
  (Yes, that is three values; “IP” and “Port” come from columns 1 and 2, “Service” from column 4.)  
• From vulns.csv you must keep ONLY the following two columns, **in this order**:  
  1) CVE  
  2) Severity  
  (These are columns 2 and 3 of vulns.csv.)  

Join rule
---------
Rows are related by the common string in the “Service” column.  If a service listed in open_ports.csv is not present in vulns.csv, that row is omitted from the final report.  (In the provided data every service is vulnerable, so four rows will be produced.)

Output artefacts to create
--------------------------
1. Directory: /home/user/report (create it if it does not exist).
2. Main report: /home/user/report/vuln_report.csv  
   • Must contain a header line followed by exactly FOUR data lines.  
   • The header line must be:  
     IP,Port,Service,CVE,Severity  
   • Sort the rows first by the IP field (ascending, as plain text) and then by the Port field (numerically ascending).  
   • Use a single comma “,” as the delimiter with **no extra spaces** anywhere in the file (even after commas or at line ends).  
   • The file must end with a trailing newline character.

3. Processing log: /home/user/report/process.log  
   The log is plain-text and must contain exactly TWO lines in this order:
   Line 1 → Parsed 4 open-port entries from /home/user/scans/open_ports.csv  
   Line 2 → Matched 4 services with known vulnerabilities from /home/user/scans/vulns.csv  
   Do not add timestamps or any additional wording.

Success criteria checked automatically
--------------------------------------
• /home/user/report/vuln_report.csv exists.  
• It has exactly 5 lines (1 header + 4 data) and **no blank lines**.  
• The header is exactly as specified.  
• The four data lines appear in the sorted order and match the expected comma-separated values.  
• /home/user/report/process.log exists with the two precise lines listed above.  

Hints
-----
• Use cut with the --delimiter=',' option and explicit field numbers.  
• Use paste –d',' to glue the cut outputs horizontally.  
• Consider using grep, comm, or join to ensure that only matching services are kept.  
• Remember to redirect program output to create /home/user/report/vuln_report.csv and /home/user/report/process.log.  

You do not need root privileges; everything is under /home/user.  Operate purely from the shell.
truth:
Pre-existing directory and files
--------------------------------
1. Directory: /home/user/scans  (permissions: 755)

2. /home/user/scans/open_ports.csv  (permissions: 644)  
   Exact content (with trailing newline):
   IP,Port,Protocol,Service
   192.168.1.10,22,tcp,ssh
   192.168.1.10,80,tcp,http
   192.168.1.20,445,tcp,smb
   192.168.1.20,3389,tcp,rdp

3. /home/user/scans/vulns.csv  (permissions: 644)  
   Exact content (with trailing newline):
   Service,CVE,Severity
   ssh,CVE-2018-15473,High
   smb,CVE-2020-0796,Critical
   http,CVE-2021-26855,High
   rdp,CVE-2019-0708,Critical

Expected artefacts after the agent finishes
-------------------------------------------
1. /home/user/report directory (permissions: 755)

2. /home/user/report/vuln_report.csv  (permissions: 644)  
   Expected exact content (with trailing newline):
   IP,Port,Service,CVE,Severity
   192.168.1.10,22,ssh,CVE-2018-15473,High
   192.168.1.10,80,http,CVE-2021-26855,High
   192.168.1.20,445,smb,CVE-2020-0796,Critical
   192.168.1.20,3389,rdp,CVE-2019-0708,Critical

3. /home/user/report/process.log  (permissions: 644)  
   Expected exact content (with trailing newline):
   Parsed 4 open-port entries from /home/user/scans/open_ports.csv
   Matched 4 services with known vulnerabilities from /home/user/scans/vulns.csv
